si.t: Go to the 'Nut_StatusTool' worksheet. Fill up the necessary information in the headings. These cells are highlighted in green.
Before entering child data, familiarize yourself with some important reminders for data entry (see below).

Refer to the paper forms (i.e. NNC Form 1A) that you used to record details of preschool children from your last OPT Plus activity. Type in all the required data for each child into the 'Nut_StatusTool' worksheet one at a time. One child, one row.

This worksheet enables you to delete names from the list of children in your OPT Plus database, based on the most recent house-to-house survey. By deleting duplicate names and children who are already older than 59 months as of the current date, you can "clean" and update the list of preschool children in your barangay or purok/ Street/Block# (depending on how this tool is being used).

• Scroll down the list of children. Children whose names are highlighted in red across the whole row are those that have been entered more than once. Once you verify that the names highlighted in red are duplicates of the same child, you can delete the extra names to ensure that the name only appears once in the list. However, a new feature of this tool is that it does not count names of children that appear more than once and excludes them from the total, as an added precaution against duplicate counts.

1. Point your cursor to the row number on the leftmost side of the screen to the left of column A. Once you see a black arrow pointing to the right, click on the mouse once to highlight the entire row you wish to delete.
2. Right click on your mouse to see the menu of options. Go to 'Delete' and click on your left mouse button to delete the row. DO NOT use the 'Delete' key on you keyboard.

1. Hold down your left mouse button to highlight all the content of the rows and columns with children's names and details. Copy all rows, columns, and cells that are highlighted in yellow. After highlighting all the cells for copying, click on your right mouse button to expand the menu of options.
2. After right-clicking on your mouse, go to the 'Copy' option to put the contents of the worksheets cells you wish to copy onto your clipboard.
4. First place the mouse cursor in cell B10. Then paste the contents of the cells copied from the "Clean&Update' (formerly 'NewEntry&Update') source worksheet.
ADDING NEW NAMES, DETAILS, AND MEASUREMENTS
2 - Click on the 'Page' tab. Under 'Orientation', choose 'Landscape'. At the bottom part of the 'Page' tab, set your paper size by choosing from the dropdown list.
SPECIFY THE NUMBER OF PAGES TO PRINT
You may email us at info@nnc.gov.ph or text us at 0918 5215166 or 0921 9660024 for your feedback or suggestions.
Raymundo Celestino F. Habito Jr. MD, MHN
IMPORTANT INFO ON DATE FORMAT ENTRY: PLS READ
Note: This worksheet enables you to update and edit/clean the existing OPT Plus master list of children in your barangay. This worksheet is not intended to be printed out.
IMPORTANT: Please read the instructions before using this worksheet.
ROWS HIGHLIGHTED IN RED: OVERAGED CHILD OR THE CHILD'S NAME HAS BEEN ENTERED MORE THAN ONCE
DO NOT COPY THIS COLUMN TO THE NUT_STATUS TOOL

r.t: Barangay Tally and Summary Sheet of Preschoolers with Weight & Height Measurement by Age Group, Sex and Nutritional Status

si.t: Use WEIGHT-FOR-LENGTH or WEIGHT-FOR-HEIGHT to correctly determine overweight and obesity
Use WEIGHT-FOR-LENGTH for 0-23 months old preschool children and WEIGHT-FOR-HEIGHT for 24-60 months old preschool children.
Name and Signature of Barangay Nutrition Scholar
Name and Signature of Midwife/Nurse/MNAO/MHO or
Name and Signature of Barangay Captain,
Celestino F. Habito Jr. MD, MHN
Note: This list can be copied to create other customized monitoring worksheets.
Total # of names in the list
NO DATA ENTRY REQUIRED RESULTS WILL BE AUTO-FILLED
Based on the WHO Child Growth Standards
WEIGHT FOR AGE, HEIGHT FOR AGE, WEIGHT FOR LENGTH/HEIGHT STATUS
Open the e-OPT Tool file that contains the entries from the most recent OPT Plus done in your barangay.
If your barangay already has e-OPT records and you are preparing for an upcoming e-OPT Plus activity
Open your barangay's most recent e-OPT Plus file that contains the list of preschool children and their details.
If your barangay has used the e-OPT Tool previously and you wish to update and complete your e-OPT master list

Get the latest version of the e-OPT Tool. Into a blank 'Nut_StatusTool' worksheet, copy and paste (starting from Row # 10) the names and details of age-eligible children from the cleaned 'Clean&Update' worksheet. Always use 'Paste>>Values' and not the simple 'Paste' option. This copy and paste step saves you from having to re-type in most of the names and details of children that have already been encoded previously.

At the end of the list that you copied from existing e-OPT files, type in the names and details of new or previously unlisted children in the barangay. This should include infants born in the past year, new residents in the barangay, and those who were not included in past OPT Plus.

Go to the 'NewEntry&Update' worksheet from your last OPT Plus. Delete any duplicate entries and the names of children older than 59 months. These are the rows that are highlighted in red. A step-by-step description is outlined in the 'Tips for Using the 'Clean&Update' worksheet shown below. Note: the 'NewEntry&Update' worksheet in earlier versions of the e-OPT Tool has been renamed to 'Clean&Update'.
For each child in the list, note any missing or incomplete details, so that the needed information can be obtained by the nutrition worker from mothers or caregivers during the next house visit.

Make a printout of the 'BNS_Printout' sheet. This serves as your pre-printed list of preschool children in your barangay which you can use to find age-eligible children as you make the rounds in the community. Names of preschoolers are alphabetically arranged to help you match children and names. You should also write down the names of new or previously unlisted children at the end of this list.

This update now includes auto-fill of population data in the Summary worksheet, as well as the auto-labelling of the Philippine Standard Geographic Code (PSGC) for each barangay. Population data (i.e. total barangay population, 0-59 months children) are from DOH - Epidemiology Bureau estimates for 2021.

Type in the YEAR (cell O1). Choose first the PROVINCE/NCR LGU, then the CITY/MUNICIPALITY, and lastly the name of the BARANGAY from the dropdown lists in each corresponding cell. Please fill these up in sequence.
HOW TO REMOVE NAMES OF CHILDREN THAT ARE DUPLICATES OR THOSE MORE THAN 59 MONTHS OLD
There are 10 print-ready worksheets in this tool. These are:
7. Type in the latest weight and height measurements of children. Make sure to enter these under the appropriate columns and spaces in the 'Nut_StatusTool'.
6. Determine the actual date when the child was measured and type this into the 'Date Measured' cell (column H).
• Summary • BNS Printout • OPT Form 1A • OPT Form 1B • List UW (Underweight) • List St (Stunted)
• List W (Wasted) • List SUW (Severely Underweight) • List SSt (Severely Stunted) • List SW (Severely Wasted)
COPYING THE CLEANED MASTERLIST TO YOUR CLIPBOARD
TRANSFERRING THE CLEANED LIST INTO A NEW BLANK e-OPT TOOL
If you are using the e-OPT Tool for the first time

If you have already encoded the names of the children in your barangay into the e-OPT Tool previously, this should save you from having to re-type in the names of most children in your barangay again the next time you do another OPT Plus activity. Subsequently, only the names and details of new children will need to be added and typed in after each annual OPT Plus.

3. Get the latest version of the e-OPT Tool from the NNC website. Open a blank copy of the e-OPT Tool and transfer the contents of what you copied in the previous step into the blank 'Nut_StatusTool' worksheet.

1 - Click on the Page Layout tab on your menu. Then click on the small arrow at the bottom right corner of the Page Setup group. The Page Setup Dialog Box with 4 tabs (Page, Margins, Header/Footer, and Sheet) should appear.
3 - Click on the 'Margins' tab. Set the margins according to the paper size you are using. Apply the following margin settings listed below:
Go to the 'BNS_Printout worksheet in that file. Type in the date of the upcoming OPT Plus or house visit in cell highlighted in green (Cell J2).

r.t: appears in Cell C3, you should use this file for a Purok or smaller part of the barangay. In Cell A3, choose the "Purok/Area/Block" option. Please then type in the name of the Purok or sub-part of the barangay in Cell C6.

You can then begin entering information for each child in the main table -- one child at a time -- starting from the first blank row (Row # 10). Or, if you already have an encoded worksheet with e-OPT data from previous use of the tool, you can batch copy the list of multiple children's names and details into the blank Nut_StatusTool using the "Copy>>Paste>>Values" function of MS Excel. Please just note that the children's names and other information from the source worksheet should be arranged in exactly the same way the columns in the tool are set up. Also make sure that the source data are pasted into the blank tool starting from Row #10 downwards.
Adaptable for use in small or large barangays.

The tool has a capacity of 1000 child entries. For barangays with more than 1000 preschool children (i.e. 0-59 months old), users can also apply this tool for smaller parts of the barangay such as Puroks, Blocks, or Areas. The data from the sub-part of the barangay can then be aggregated and summarized as a single file using another e-OPT Tool that consolidates the barangay data (e-OPT Tool 1A - 'e-OPT Tool for Large Barangays').

The need for re-typing all the names of preschool children in the barangay and entering their details after every annual OPT Plus activity can be avoided, as the e-OPT Tool Template can just be updated, exported, renamed, and saved into a new file every year, or as often as needed.
Lists of affected children are auto-generated.

The tool was designed to make tabulation and report preparation by nutrition workers less tedious and more timely. This feature is expected to significantly lessen the amount of time previously spent by nutrition workers in preparing OPT Plus reports using the old paper-and-pen method. It also lessens the chances for manual calculation errors and discrepancies during the tabulation of summary results. With faster turnaround time,

children found to have moderate and severe malnutrition in any of the three indices are can now be identified and listed separately so that these children can be prioritized, visited or referred, and given appropriate treatment or support by the LGU.

The lists of affected children generated by the tool can also be copied and transferred to another MS Excel worksheet. This allows users to create more detailed or customized monitoring formats depending on the needs of health and nutrition workers in the locality.

During community surveys, names of children can be inadvertantly included in the OPT list more than once. To avoid errors in tallying and reporting the data, the tool has been set up to automatically exclude duplicate names and the numbers from these repeated entries are not included in the totals in the summary results. Once these names are validated to be from one individual child, users can then just delete the repeated names later when they clean or update the master list of children in their community.
DATE FORMAT TO USE IN ENTERING DATES.

First determine the date format settings on your computer at the bottom right corner of your screen on your taskbar (or you can look at the date format in cell K1 in 'Nut_StatusTool'). For example, if your computer's date format is MM-DD-YYYY, "January 21, 2021" would appear as "01/21/2021"; if it is set as DD-MM-YYYY, it would appear as "21-01-2021".
The way you type in the date format into the tool for the child's date of birth and the date of measurement

as the way the date format in your computer's operating system appears (e.g. MM/DD/YYYY or DD/MM/YYYY). Always type in any date using the format consistent with your computer's settings. When you type in the numeric date, make sure that the number for the month and the date are not interchanged, otherwise, this will lead to incorrect calculation of the child's age in months. After you have typed in the numeric date, it will then appear in the cell as MMM-DD-YYY (for example: Jan-21-2021), regardless of your computer's date format settings. This is to help users in reading the correct dates after the date has been typed in. Please also check that the date and time settings of your computer are always correct.
• Reminder: When copying from another worksheet into the e-OPT Tool, always use the option
. Doing this avoids errors that might happen as underlying formulas and formats in cells from the source will also be copied if just "Paste" is used.

si.t: • Children who are older than 59 months are also highlighted in red. You can also remove these names from the list as you clean and update your list of 0-59 months old children.

r.t: Once there are no more remaining rows highlighted in red, you can then proceed to copy the contents of the entire worksheet under
Note: To paste, right click on your mouse and

. (Caution: if you use just the plain "Paste" option, you will be copying not only the content of the cells but also any underlying formulas and formatting that came from the source worksheet. This often leads to copying errors and incorrect data.)
Note: These margin settings allow for placement of fasteners or binders for the printed sheets
for Letter and A4 paper sizes and
for Long and Legal size paper .
The printable worksheets have been formatted for A4 size paper, Landscape orientation.
However, you can also use other paper sizes, depending on what is available in your LGU.
You will just need to adjust the page margins and scaling settings as described below.
6 - Click on File >> Print. The Printer Dialog Box/Settings should appear. Set the number of page to print in Page Range and select Active Sheet(s) under 'Print what'. (E.g. Pages ___

si.t: 7 - Click on the 'OK' or 'Print' button when ready.
Number of Children with Incomplete Information
Before you Begin - Important Reminders for Use of the Nut_StatusTool Worksheet:
CHOOSE FROM DROPDOWN LIST TO FILL IN PROVINCE >> MUNICIPALITY/CITY >> BARANGAY IN SEQUENCE. IF USING FOR A PUROK, PLS TYPE IN PUROK NAME
Tip: To adjust the size of the worksheet to fit your screen, use the zoom level slider at the right bottom corner below. Depending on the size of your screen, set the zoom level between 60-90%.
Note: This is a printable worksheet. Before printing, please read printing instructions (click on the link).
INSTRUCTIONS: To copy the entire row of data: Select/Highlight the entire row of data by doing these steps:
1) First select cell B3 (the cell with the name of the Barangay or Purok), highlighted in light green.
start copying from the cell below
No. of Children w/ Valid WFA
No. of Children w/ Valid HFA
No. of Children w/ Valid WFL/H

1/ Refers to previous year prevalence rate of the area
1/ Refers to previous year prevalence rate of the area, for comparison with current year

r.t: Barangay Tally and Summary Sheet of Preschoolers with Weight & Height Measurement by Age Group, Sex and Nutritional Status

si.t: Estimated Popn of Children 0-59 mos in Barangay:

This tool is for use by nutrition workers in the community to help them record, interpret, and summarize results of the OPT Plus -- an annual activity that is done in barangays across the country to measure the nutritional status of children 0-59 months old. The e-OPT Tool runs on MS Excel version 2007 or later. Full functionality of the tool requires a licensed version of MS Excel.
Prevalence Rate UW & SUW (Last OPT Plus).
Prevalence Rate Wasting & Sev. Wasting (Last OPT Plus):
Prevalence Rate Stunting & Sev. Stunting (Last OPT Plus):
Year Covered by this OPT Plus:
WEIGHT FOR AGE, HEIGHT FOR AGE, & WEIGHT FOR LENGTH/HEIGHT STATUS

r.t: Take note of the total number of pages to print as indicated in the worksheet you are printing. For example, in the lists of children listed as UW,St,W, SUW, St, and SW, this number is shown in
. For the BNS_Printout, the number of pages for printing is shown in
. The Summary worksheet is only 1 page.

si.t: Purok or Location in the Barangay
Date of the Next OPT Plus Visit:
Before printing, please read printing instructions (click on the link).
Purok, Block #, Area or Location in the Barangay
Form 1B. List of Affected/At-risk Preschool Children 0-59 Months Old
LIST OF UNDERWEIGHT CHILDREN 0-59 MONTHS OLD
LIST OF STUNTED CHILDREN 0-59 MONTHS OLD
LIST OF SEVERELY UNDERWEIGHT CHILDREN 0-59 MONTHS OLD
LIST OF SEVERELY STUNTED CHILDREN 0-59 MONTHS OLD
LIST OF SEVERELY WASTED CHILDREN 0-59 MONTHS OLD
# Children with weight but no height:
# Children with height but no weight:
Summary of Children covered by e-OPT Plus
This counts the number of children between 0 and 59 months of age whose nutritional status falls below any of the normal cut-offs for weight for age, height for age, or weight for length/height.
This counts the number of children between 0 and 23 months of age whose nutritional status falls below any of the normal cut-offs for weight for age, height for age, or weight for length/height.

Counts the number of listed children 0-59 months old who are missing any of the following: address or location of residence, name of mother/caregiver, IP status (whether or not child belongs to an indigenous group), child's sex, date of birth or date measured. Children with incomplete information are flagged with gray color in Column A of Nut_StatusTool.

Note: a) R1 means Row No. 1, R2 means Row 2, etc.; b)Total (R7) - refers to the sum of preschoolers by nutritional status for children 0-59 months; c) Prevalence (R8 & R10)- refers to the prevalence rate by sex, by nutritional status for age group 0- 59 mos.

Note: a) R1 means Row No. 1, R2 means Row 2, etc.; b)Total (R7) - refers to the sum of preschoolers by nutritional status for children 0-59 months; c) Prevalence (R8 & R10)- refers to the prevalence rate by sex, by nutritional status for age group 0-59 mos.
Name and Signature of Barangay Captain
Estimated Population of Children 0-59 months old:
Estimated Popn of Children 0-59 months old:
Estimated Population of Children 0-59 months old:
Number of Children with Missing Weight and/or Height Measurements
Number of Mothers/Caregivers of Children Affected by Overweight ot Obesity
Counts the number of mothers/caregivers who has at least one child who falls above the normal cut-off for weight for age or weight for length/height.
Number of Mothers/Caregivers of Children Affected by Undernutrition
Number of children 0-23 months old Affected by Undernutrition
Counts the number of mothers/caregivers who have at least one child who falls below the normal cut-off for weight for age, height for age, or weight for length/height.

Counts the number of listed children 0-59 months old who do not have either weight or height measurements. Children with incomplete measurements are flagged with a yellow-shaded cell in the corresponding cell with the missing measurement (Column I or J).
Number of children 0-59 months old Affected by Undernutrition
Number of children 0-59 months oldwho are Overweight or Obese
This counts the number of children between 0 and 59 months of age whose nutritional status falls above any of the normal cut-offs for weight for age or weight for length/height.
Number of children whose names are repeated more than once; Number of children older than 59 months.

If a name of child is repeated and written in exactly the same way, this is detected by the tool as a duplicate and only the first occurrence is included in the total tally in the Summary table. Similarly, children who are older than 59 months are excluded by the tool from the total counts.
Please add the names and details of new or unlisted children at the end of this list.
Note: For new children, just get the birthdate. Leave this space blank.

The counts of mothers/caregivers of children classified as having undernutrition as well as overweight and obesity have been included in the Summary. This counts the number of mothers/caregivers who have at least one child whose nutritional status falls below the lower cut-offs for normal WFA, HFA, or WFL/H and those who have children classified as overweight or obese (WFA or WFL/H). These counts are intended to help LGUs in nutrition program planning.
Note: if a mother/caregiver has more than one child affected, she/he is counted only once.

r.t: By typing in the child's weight and height, as well as the date of birth and the date of measurement, the tool quickly calculates a child's age in months and auto-generates the nutritional status. The classification is based on the WHO Child Growth Standards for the three indices weight for age, height for age, and weight for length/height. After all the children's measurements are taken, these are recorded then later fed into the tool by the nutrition worker or others in a local facility with access to a computer. This automatic feature is intended to make it quicker for nutrition workers to collect and interpret the measurements of many children in the community. This could also lessen the chances of errors compared with the previous approach where -- after each child's measurements are taken -- the nutrition worker had to manually look up each child's nutritional status from three kinds of printed reference tables, write down each result in a recording sheet, and repeat the process for all children visited.

si.t: LIST OF MODERATELY WASTED CHILDREN 0-59 MONTHS OLD
Prevalence Rate Moderate Wasting & Severe Wasting:

A count of the number of children with either the weight or height measurement missing or those with incomplete information has been included in the Summary. For 'incomplete information', this reflects the number of children who are missing any of the following: home address, name of mother/caregiver, IP status, sex, date of birth, or date of measurement. To help users in spotting the children with missing details in the 'Nut_StatusTool', the name is flagged with a gray colored cell in the 'Child Seq.' column (Column A). Also,iIf any of these information (except for home address) are not filled in, this will result in non-appearance of the child's nutritional status.

The tally of children belonging to indigenous peoples (IP) groups according to their nutritional status classification in each of the three indices has been added to the summary table. The total presented is also disaggregated by sex.

r.t: Always start entering child data in Row # 10. After you have started data entry in the Nut_StatusTool,
do not skip rows or leave any rows blank --
this will cause your BNS Printout and the Clean&Update worksheets to not work properly. It will also result in sorting problems in your other worksheets and your reports will not appear correctly.

si.t: 5. Type in the names of new children (those born during the past year or those who are new residents in the barangay) at the end of the list of old names in the 'Nut_StatusTool' worksheet.
Additional columns have been added for children 0-23 months old in the Summary table to show the total number and prevalence in each of the three nutritional status indices WFA, HFA, and WFL/H.
Note: Specifying the total number of pages to be printed avoids pages without names being printed inadvertently and paper wastage.

The Nut_StatusTool is the main data entry worksheet. You can enter information one child at a time into each row or you can do batch entry for many children (if you already have previous worksheets) by using 'copy>>paste values' from another file.

r.t: ENSURE ALL CELLS OR INFORMATION ARE FILLED IN FOR EACH CHILD.

To auto-generate each child's nutritional status for all three indices, the following information must be complete: the name of the mother/caregiver, the child's name, IP status (whether or not the child belongs to an indigenous group), child's sex, date of birth, date measured, weight (in kg), and height (in cm). To facilitate follow-up visits by nutrition workers, the address or the location in the barangay where the child lives should also be entered. This can be the name of the purok, block, or name of the part of the barangay. If the child is missing any essential information (e.g. name of mother/caregiver), type in "TBD", and make sure to obtain the needed informationduring the next house visit. Otherwise if any details are left blank in the tool, one or all nutritional status indices for that child might not appear. To help users of the tool to quickly see if a child has no weight and/or height entered in the Nut_StatusTool, the cell for the corresponding measurement that is missing will be highlighted in yellow. If other child information is missing, the cell opposite the child's name in Column A will be highlighted with a gray color.
4 - Click on the Page tab once again. Here you can adjust the scaling for your printout. Depending on your printer settings and the paper size you are using,
set the scaling to any value between 80 - 100%.
Click on Print Preview to see if the scaling settings you selected will make your printout fit properly in your chosen paper size. If not, adjust the scaling accordingly.

si.t: # M/Cs of 0-59 mos children affected by Undernutrition:

Total Number of M/Cs of children 0-23 mos. old:

Number of Children Affected by Undernutrition:
Number of Children with Overweight or Obesity:
0-59 mos. old w/ overwt or Ob
# 0-23 mos. old w/ Below Normal NS
# M/Cs of 0-59m w/ Below N child
# M/Cs of 0-59m w/ Overwt or Ob child
# M/Cs of 0-23m old w/ Below N child

2) Then press the keys 'Shift + Ctrl' --> 'Right Arrow'. This should select all the cells that contain data in the entire row up to column GV. Scroll to the right to make sure all the cells from B3 to GV3 have been selected.

3) Copy all the data in cells B3-GV3 by either a) right clicking on your mouse and selecting 'Copy' or by b) pressing 'Ctrl +C' on your keyboard. The cells you copied should now be in your clipboard - ready for pasting into the e-OPT Consolidation Tool file.
For a maximum of 1000 children in a small or medium sized barangay. For large barangays, use this file for a purok, section, or part of the barangay.
Helper for Conditional Format of Worksheet

r.t: FILL IN ALL THE REQUIRED INFORMATION IN THE HEADINGS SECTION.

Before you start entering children's data, first fill up all cells that are highlighted in green. The data entry worksheet will not appear if there are any green-highlighted cells left blank. Filling up these cells ensures that the headings in all tables and reports in other parts of this tool are filled in.

. Doinhg this will hide the ribbon above the worksheet and will give you more working space as you enter data. You can toggle this on or off. Use CTRL + Fn + F1 for keyboards or laptops that have a Fn key.

The contents of cells from B3 to GV3 shown above are meant to be exported for consolidation to either a large barangay (in areas where the barangay preschool population exceeds 1000 children) or to the municipal or city-level data. The data in this entire row of cells should be copied and pasted into the appropriate consolidation tool. If you used this tool for a purok or section of the barangay, you should then consolidate data using the e-OPT Plus Tool for Large Barangays. If you used this tool for an entire barangay, you should consolidate your data to the municipal or city level. You can download the latest versions of these tools from the NNC website.
Please note that the consolidation tools for the e-OPT Acute Malnutrition Tool are different from the 'Regular' e-OPT Plus Tool.
If you have any questions, please contact the Nutrition Surveillance Division to ensure that you are using the appropriate consolidation tools.

si.t: BANGSAMORO AUTONOMOUS REGION IN MUSLIM MINDANAO BARMM
CITY OF SAN JOSE DEL MONTE
